history.content:
<thought>
The goal is not visible in this local view. I see walls (#) forming a vertical barrier at column C. I am currently facing unknown, but the cell directly in front of me is part of the open area near the bottom (row 07 shows open cells to the right at columns E–G). To start moving toward the more open region while avoiding the lava/blocked column, I will first rotate clockwise to search for a safe forward path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I can see my current position at row 07 column D (the cell with ^). I also see a wall at row 07 column E (#) so moving forward to the right (if I were facing east) would be blocked. The safe open area appears to the left at row 07 column A/B (.) and also at row 06 column D (.). To move toward open cells, I will turn left to face west and then move forward if possible.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m still at row 07 column D (with ^). In front of me appears to be the cell at row 06 column D, which is lava (L) based on row 06 showing L at column D. That means moving forward would be deadly. To avoid lava, I should rotate so that I can move sideways into a safe floor cell.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D (^). The cell directly in front is now row 07 column E, which is a wall (#), so I can’t move forward. I should turn left to face north toward row 06 column D, which is lava (L), but turning left would make forward correspond to that lava; instead I’ll turn right again to face west (row 07 column C is .) and then move forward into the safe cell.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
My orientation seems to have shifted from the last view. In the current view, I am at row 07 column D (^) and I can see walls (#) across row 06 (all #), so moving forward into row 06 is impossible/dangerous. The cell in front is row 07 column E which is # (wall), so I cannot move forward. The safest option is to turn left to try an open adjacent cell on row 07 (toward column C which is .).
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 07 column D facing north (^) in this view. The goal isn’t visible yet. I can see lava (L) directly in front at row 06 column D, so I should not move forward. I’ll turn right to face east toward row 07 column E, which is a wall (#), so I’ll then turn left next to head west toward row 07 column C (.)—so now I’ll start by turning right to adjust orientation away from lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
In this new local view, row 06 is entirely walls (#), so moving forward from my position at row 07 column D will be impossible if I face north. The cell in front when facing north would be row 06 column D which is #. I should turn to face east or west where row 07 has floor at columns C and B (both shown as .) and column E is # (blocked). I’ll turn left so I face west (toward row 07 column C which is .).
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m back to the same position/orientation as earlier: row 07 column D with ^ (facing north). The cell directly in front (row 06 column D) is '.' so moving forward is safe and may lead toward the goal. I will move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is not visible yet. My current cell is row 07 column D facing north (^). The cell in front (row 06 column D) is '.' so I can move forward safely and continue exploring the open vertical corridor.

<action>

</action>

<thought>

</thought>
<action>

</action>

<thought>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing north (^). The cell in front (row 06 column D) is 'L' (lava), so I cannot move forward. I should turn to find a safe path; turning right will face east toward an open area (row 07 column E is '#', so east is blocked), but turning left will face west where row 07 column C is '.' and safe. Therefore I will turn left.
</thought>
<action>

<thought>

<action>
